title: Molten Croatian Butter Frittata
description: A buttery and rich Croatian-inspired breakfast dish featuring shio-koji and tempering chocolate, perfect for a lazy Sunday morning.

ingredients.name: Shio-Koji
ingredients.notes: Japanese fermented soybean paste for umami flavor

ingredients.name: Tempering Chocolate
ingredients.notes: Dark chocolate with at least 70% cocoa solids for added depth

ingredients.name: Heavy Cream
ingredients.notes: Whole milk is okay, but heavy cream is preferred

ingredients.name: Unsalted Butter
ingredients.notes: Melted to perfection for a rich frittata

ingredients.name: Fresh Parsley
ingredients.notes: Chopped for garnish, but don't overdo it

instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit and grease a 9-inch skillet with butter.
Whisk together eggs, heavy cream, and a pinch of salt in a large bowl.
Add shio-koji, tempering chocolate, garlic, and red pepper flakes to the egg mixture and stir gently to combine.
Pour the egg mixture into the prepared skillet and let cook for 5 minutes over medium heat.
Transfer the skillet to the oven and bake for 10-12 minutes, or until the eggs are almost set and the top is golden brown.
Remove the skillet from the oven and let it cool for 1-2 minutes.
Sprinkle with fresh parsley and serve immediately, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
